headline: Agility Robotics first agent baseline. Established-band placement reinforced by compound positive evidence: (1) Toyota Motor Manufacturing Canada commercial agreement February 19 2026 — 7 Digit robots deployed at Woodstock, Ontario plant under RaaS contract for RAV4 logistics; first commercial humanoid deployment in Canadian automotive industry following year-long evaluation pilot; (2) Agility was a 2022 anti-weaponization pledge signatory (alongside Boston Dynamics, ANYbotics, Clearpath, Open Robotics, Unitree); (3) ASTM safety standards working group participation with Boston Dynamics and Figure AI; (4) public commitment to 'first cooperatively safe humanoid robot to work alongside people.' Published 60.9 / Established band sustained. Half-step up on ACT (operational-deployment maturity) and SYS (safety-standards leadership) offset by EQU sub-threshold drift (RaaS contract structure raises labor-displacement equity questions for Toyota workers — sub-threshold). Net composite drift: +1.5 points; sub-threshold. Recommend confirm at 60.9 with first-baseline rationale.
key_evidence: February 19 2026: Agility Robotics announced commercial agreement with Toyota Motor Manufacturing Canada — 7 Digit humanoids deployed at Woodstock, Ontario plant under Robots-as-a-Service (RaaS) contract for RAV4 SUV production logistics. First commercial humanoid deployment in Canadian automotive industry.
Year-long evaluation pilot (3 phases: development, proof-of-technology, live onsite) preceded commercial conversion — structured deployment process is BND/ACT-positive.
Agility 2022 anti-weaponization pledge signatory: pledged not to weaponize nor allow customer weaponization of robots; alongside Boston Dynamics, ANYbotics, Clearpath, Open Robotics, Unitree.
ASTM humanoid safety standards working group participant alongside Boston Dynamics and Figure AI; May 27-28 2026 Boston convening upcoming.
Public commitment to 'first cooperatively safe humanoid robot to work alongside people' (Toyota deployment release) — explicit safety-design framing.
Pre-existing commercial deployments at GXO, Spanx Inc.; Digit operating without weaponization or surveillance-deployment record.

evidence.source: Agility Robotics — Commercial Agreement with Toyota Motor Manufacturing Canada
evidence.finding: First commercial humanoid deployment in Canadian automotive industry; year-long evaluation pilot preceded commercial conversion; cooperatively-safe-humanoid framing.

evidence.source: The Robot Report — Toyota Motor Manufacturing Canada to deploy Agility Robotics' Digit humanoids
evidence.finding: RaaS contract structure; 7 Digit units active; RAV4 logistics application.

evidence.source: TechCrunch — Toyota contracts seven Agility humanoid robots for Canadian factory
evidence.finding: Commercial deployment scope confirmed; structured pilot-to-commercial conversion process.

evidence.source: The Robot Report — Agility, Boston Dynamics, ASTM to discuss state of humanoid robotics
evidence.finding: ASTM humanoid safety standards working group participation; cross-vendor safety-standards leadership.

evidence.source: Fortune — 6 major robot makers anti-weaponization pledge

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3.5 confirmed. A1 detection: structured pilot-to-commercial process surfaces operational issues; A4 amplification: ASTM standards-working-group participation amplifies cross-vendor safety signals.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 3.5 confirmed. E1 affective resonance: 'cooperatively safe humanoid' framing acknowledges human-coexistence concern.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3.5 confirmed. AC2 proportionality: year-long evaluation pilot preceded Toyota commercial conversion; AC4 response framework integrated into deployment.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 3.0 confirmed. EQ2 distributional awareness: RaaS contract structure raises labor-displacement equity questions for Toyota workers (sub-threshold drift); however, no documented displacement events.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3.5 confirmed. B3 scope clarity: anti-weaponization pledge active; B5 consent: deployment context (factory logistics) consistent with declared use cases.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 3.5 confirmed. AB2 transparency: commercial-agreement and pilot-process disclosure functional; AB4 reparation: RaaS contract structure provides commercial-level accountability framework.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.5 confirmed. S1 causal reasoning: ASTM standards-working-group participation indicates systemic-harm reasoning at industry level; structured pilot process applies systemic reasoning at deployment level.
scoring_rationale.INT: 3.5 confirmed. I1 consistency: anti-weaponization pledge (2022) maintained through 2026 commercial deployment; declared safety-first commitment consistent with operational pattern.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstruction confirms 60.9. Established band sustained. Confirm at 60.9 with first-baseline rationale; sub-threshold positive drift documented but not score-changing.

negativeSignals: RaaS contract structure raises labor-displacement equity questions (sub-threshold); humanoid commercial-deployment pre-dates EU Machinery Regulation (2027) and OSHA-humanoid guidance (2027) — regulatory-gap structural risk; no published independent third-party safety audit; pricing/contract terms not fully transparent.
positiveSignals: Toyota Motor Manufacturing Canada commercial agreement with year-long evaluation pilot; first commercial humanoid in Canadian automotive industry; 2022 anti-weaponization pledge signatory; ASTM humanoid safety standards working group participation; 'cooperatively safe humanoid' framing; pre-existing commercial deployments at GXO and Spanx without weaponization/surveillance record.
sector_context: Robotics Labs / commercial-humanoid cluster. Cross-reference: Boston Dynamics (anti-weaponization pledge co-signatory, established band, Hyundai industrial deployment), Figure AI (BMW Spartanburg pilot), Apptronik (Mercedes/GXO/Jabil pilots), 1X Technologies (NEO consumer pre-orders), Tesla Optimus (no pledge, factory deployment). Agility distinguished by structured pilot-to-commercial process + anti-weaponization pledge + ASTM leadership.
open_actions_resolved: First baseline established with established-band confirmation
Toyota Canada Feb 2026 commercial deployment absorbed
ASTM safety standards working group participation registered
open_actions_carried: Watch: ASTM May 27-28 2026 Boston convening outcomes
Watch: Toyota Canada deployment scaling and labor-impact reporting
Watch: 2027 EU Machinery Regulation and OSHA-humanoid guidance compliance posture
Watch: third-party independent safety audit emergence
Watch: any humanoid-deployment harm or incident report
